note_on_two_pulls: ApeWisdom was fetched twice this run ~6 minutes apart (live feed, mentions shift in real time). Both pulls' qualified candidates are kept -- nothing is discarded just because a later snapshot reshuffled the accelerating list.

qualified_set.note: Leveraged South Korea 3x ETF, not a single-name equity -- would be vetoed on merits (no insiders/catalysts possible) even had it confirmed.

recommendations.catalyst: 8-K filed 2026-06-22: Capital Equipment Purchase Agreement with Fit Energy USA LP for up to 380MW of carbonate fuel cell 'Blocks' supplying baseload power to data centers, phased over 4 stages (phase 0 = 30MW committed now, phases 1-3 optional on Fit's election). Fit also received 12M performance-vesting warrants at $26.44 strike tied to its own deposit milestones -- incentive-aligned, not a cash raise.
recommendations.key_risk: Stock already ran from ~$21 (6/25) to ~$36 (6/30), most of it in the last 2 sessions -- a large chunk of the move likely already happened before this shortlist. StockTwits chatter is heavy squeeze/meme language ('Apes', 'low float... hit circuit breakers', 'shorty beware') layered on top of the real catalyst, so part of the move is speculative momentum, not just the news. Only phase 0 (30MW) of the 380MW is contractually committed; phases 1-3 are Fit's option. The 12M warrants are real future dilution once vested/exercised. No fresh Form 4 insider buying in the surge window (last filing 6/15, pre-catalyst) -- no insider corroboration either way.
recommendations.note: Sole recommended name this week. LUNR also accelerated and sits in the small/mid band but failed the volume-confirmation gate (price +14% on volume BELOW its 20-day baseline) so it is excluded from recommendations per the mechanical gate -- flagged for the ghost's unconfirmed control group only.

recommendations.authenticity_notes: 4 distinct low-follower StockTwits accounts (2248, 99, 3, 63 followers) posted near-identical 'revenue $250M->$1.09B, long-duration compounder' talking points within a 26-minute window (18:55-19:21 UTC). Same canned thesis, multiple small accounts, tight time cluster -- a textbook coordinated-promotion signature, not organic conviction. Chatter isolated to StockTwits; no second-platform corroboration found.
recommendations.direction: bull (per the promotional posts) but unsupported
recommendations.catalyst: None found. Most recent 8-K (2026-06-23) is a routine credit-facility amendment (Amendment No. 4: commitment cut $100M->$50M, borrowing margin reduced) -- balance-sheet housekeeping, not a growth catalyst, and doesn't match the 'revenue transformation' narrative in chat.
recommendations.insider_check: CEO Caryn Seidman-Becker's two most recent Form 4s (2026-06-26, 2026-06-29) are option exercises followed by sell-to-cover / open-market sales (codes M then S/F) -- net insider SELLING. No open-market insider purchases (code P) in the trailing 3 weeks.
recommendations.key_risk: Seeded-looking chatter with no fundamental trigger and an insider selling into the move -- high odds of fade once the promotional push tapers.
recommendations.thesis: Cleared the mechanical gates (price +4.8%, volume 1.79x) but the LLM read finds a coordinated-looking promotional cluster with no real catalyst and net insider selling. Veto.

recommendations.authenticity_notes: Chatter spread across many distinct low-follower accounts (no single obvious cluster like YOU), but uniformly euphoric/ramp-style ('squeeze coming', 'look out above tomorrow', '$10 this year', 'more shares traded than SpaceX'). Opendoor has a long public history as a serial retail meme name with recurring hype-and-fade cycles unrelated to fundamentals.
recommendations.catalyst: No fresh 8-K -- most recent company filing was 2026-06-12, three weeks before this chatter spike. One StockTwits post cites a 'Q2 revenue $1.2B, +60%' figure attributed to unspecified 'social media chatter' -- an unverified rumor, not a company disclosure.
recommendations.insider_check: Insider David Benson sold 40,000 shares on 2026-06-16 near $4.83 (open-market sale). Two other insiders (Eric Wu, Keith Rabois) received routine equity award grants ($0 price) on 2026-06-15 -- not open-market purchases. No insider buying found; the one open-market transaction found was a sale.
recommendations.key_risk: Real price/volume move (vol 2.77x, +7.4%) but no confirmed catalyst behind it, hype-heavy language consistent with a chase/pump dynamic, and an insider sold into the same window. High reversal risk if the rumor doesn't materialize.
recommendations.thesis: Cleared the mechanical gates on real volume, but the catalyst is an unverified rumor, chat tone reads as momentum-chasing, and the only insider activity this cycle is a sale. Veto.
